title: Business Analyst
description: **Job Description:** Our client, a leading financial services organisation, is seeking an experienced Business Analyst to support the delivery of strategic business and technology initiatives. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated professional to work across key stakeholders and contribute to business transformation programmes. **Job Responsibilities** * Gather, analyse and document business requirements from stakeholders across multiple business functions. * Work closely with business users, project managers and technical teams to define solutions that meet organisational objectives. * Facilitate workshops, meetings and stakeholder discussions to elicit requirements and achieve project consensus. * Produce high\-quality business requirements documents, process maps, functional specifications and user stories. * Support the full project lifecycle from requirements gathering through to testing and implementation. * Identify opportunities for process improvements and operational efficiencies. * Assist with user acceptance testing (UAT), ensuring solutions meet agreed business requirements. * Provide regular updates to stakeholders and contribute to project governance activities. **Experience Required** * Minimum 3\+ years' experience working as a Business Analyst. * Previous experience within the Financial Services sector. * Proven ability to gather, document and manage business and functional requirements. * Strong stakeholder management skills with experience engaging across multiple levels of an organisation. * Experience supporting technology, transformation or business change projects. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Experience working within Agile, Waterfall or hybrid delivery environments. **Desirable Skills** * Experience with business process mapping and process improvement initiatives. * Knowledge of regulatory and compliance requirements within Financial Services. * Exposure to data analysis and reporting tools. * Familiarity with project management methodologies and change management frameworks. * Experience working with Jira, Confluence or similar collaboration tools. * Understanding of digital transformation and operational change programmes. **Educational Requirements** * Bachelor's Degree or equivalent third\-level qualification in Business, Finance, Information Technology or a related discipline. **HOW TO APPLY:** If you are interested in this role, please apply for this role with your updated CV

title: Business Development Associate
description: **Your role** ============= **What you'll be doing** ======================== **What We Need** Corpay is currently looking to hire a Business Development Associate within our Cross\-border division and is located in Dublin, IE. As a Business Development Associate, you will play a key role in driving new client acquisition across the Irish market. Working closely with senior sales professionals, you will identify and engage prospective customers, build strong commercial relationships, and help businesses optimise their international payments and foreign exchange requirements. You will report directly to the MD Ireland and regularly collaborate with business development teams. This role is ideal for ambitious, commercially driven individuals looking to develop a successful career within fintech, FX and B2B sales. **How We Work** As a Business Development Associate you will be expected to work in a hybrid environment. Corpay will set you up for success by providing: * Assigned workspace in Dublin. * Company\-issued equipment. * Formal, hands\-on training. **Role Responsibilities** The responsibilities of the role will include: * Conducting thorough research on target accounts in Ireland to uncover industry challenges and tailor Corpay Cross Border solutions. * Executing personalised outreach activities daily via calls, email, LinkedIn, and social channels. * Qualifying inbound and outbound leads by assessing revenue, budget, pain points, job role and timeline. * Mapping decision\-makers and internal champions, then secure discovery meetings with key stakeholders. * Scheduling and prepping high\-value sessions for Business Development Managers, providing detailed briefing notes to demonstrate our Corpay Cross Border offering. * Maintaining Salesforce with every touchpoint, lead status update, forecast and insight for real\-time pipeline visibility. * Monitoring and analysing your KPI’s/ activity levels, conversion rates and pipeline value, then optimise tactics based on data and coaching. **Qualifications \& Skills** * Minimum 1\-2 years of experience in outbound sales, lead generation and prospecting (SDR/BDR) * A fearless communicator — comfortable cold calling, emailing, and building relationships fast. * Tech\-adept: Salesforce (or similar CRM), LinkedIn Sales Navigator, and Microsoft Office come naturally. * Self\-motivated, resilient, and hungry to learn. * A natural networker with strong LinkedIn and social selling skills. **Pay Transparency** The salary range for this position is €35,000 – €42,000 gross per year. The final offer will be determined based on objective, gender\-neutral criteria including relevant skills, professional experience, and qualifications. Corpay maintains structured compensation practices and regularly reviews pay to support compliance with equal pay principles and applicable EU legislation. Additional compensation elements, including benefits and variable pay (if applicable), will be discussed during the recruitment process. \#LI\-A01 \#LI\-Hybrid **About Corpay** ================ **About Corpay** Corpay is a global technology organization that is leading the future of commercial payments with a culture of innovation that drives us to constantly create new and better ways to pay. Our specialized payment solutions help businesses control, simplify, and secure payment for fuel, general payables, toll and lodging expenses. Millions of people in over 80 countries around the world use our solutions for their payments. At Corpay, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and respectful workplace where employees are valued for their diverse perspectives, experiences, and contributions. We believe that diversity, equity, and inclusion strengthen our teams, drive innovation, and support our continued success globally. As part of our hiring process, offers of employment may be subject to the successful completion of pre\-employment screening conducted by an authorized third\-party provider, in accordance with applicable laws and Corpay policies. Screening requirements may include employment references, identity verification, criminal record checks, financial or sanctions screening, and other background checks relevant to the role and permitted by local law. **Notice to Recruitment Agencies and Search Firms:** Corpay does not accept unsolicited resumes from agencies or search firms without a valid written agreement in place. Any unsolicited candidate submissions will become the property of Corpay, and no fees will be paid related to such submissions. Learn more about Corpay: https://www.corpay.com **Transparency \& Compliance** **Equal Opportunity Employer** Corpay is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, ancestry, age, disability, marital status, genetic information, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. Corpay is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace where individuals are respected and valued for their diverse perspectives, experiences, and contributions. If you require reasonable accommodation during any part of the application or interview process, please notify a representative of the Human Resources Department. **Use of Artificial Intelligence in Hiring** Corpay may use artificial intelligence (AI) and other technology\-enabled tools to support certain aspects of the recruitment process, such as application screening, candidate assessment, or interview scheduling. These tools are designed to enhance efficiency, consistency, and fairness throughout the hiring process. AI tools do not make final hiring decisions. All employment decisions involve human review. Corpay is committed to the responsible use of AI, including appropriate oversight and safeguards designed to support fair and unbiased outcomes. **Candidate Privacy Notice** For information about how Corpay processes personal information during the recruitment process, please review our Candidate Privacy Notice: https://www.corpay.com/privacy\-policy. **Pay Philosophy** Corpay is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent compensation practices. Compensation decisions are based on objective, job\-related factors including skills, experience, qualifications, and market benchmarks. Where required by applicable law, salary or compensation ranges will be included in the job posting or provided prior to the interview process, where required by applicable law. Additional compensation elements such as bonuses, incentives, benefits, or variable pay may apply where applicable.

title: Business Operations Analyst
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* Job Category Operations Job Details **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. As a member of the The ideal candidate will be a proactive, result\-oriented team player who thrives in high\-energy settings and demonstrates a steadfast commitment to professional excellence. Business Operations team, you will work with cross\-functional teams to help provide stellar support to our internal and external stakeholders. The right candidate will be a self\-starter, action\-oriented and comfortable navigating infrastructural challenges and competing priorities. You are a highly motivated team player with expertise working in a fast paced environment and you have a proven track record on delivering results and getting things done. The successful candidate will possess the ability to work in a fast paced environment, have cross\-functional relationships management skills, communication skills and strong work ethic with a can\-do attitude. **Responsibilities:** * Direct end\-to\-end partner operations: onboarding, deal registration, referrals, quoting, and reseller support * Manage partner account setups and cross\-system data integrity * Act as the primary contact for partner inquiries and operational escalations * Resolve or redirect internal operational issues * Enforce compliance with partner program policies and guidelines * Mitigate operational risks and implement scalable process improvements * Produce SOPs, training materials, and technical documentation * Drive automation and system enhancements for efficiency Monitor SLAs, KPIs, and overall performance metrics * **Key Competencies** * Process\-oriented and operationally disciplined * Strong sense of ownership and accountability * Solutions\-oriented mindset with a proactive approach * Ability to navigate ambiguity and drive structure * Collaborative and adaptable team player Data\-driven decision making and analytical thinking * **Experience/Skills Required:** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent combination of education and experience * Relevant Channel or Sales Operations support experience * Experience in a fast\-paced environment with excellent interpersonal, problem solving and time management skills. * Extreme attention to detail with a strategic eye * Analytical thinking and driving operational excellence and improvement Demonstrated participation in process improvement initiatives and/or project management experience * **Other Requirements/Nice to have:** * Salesforce.com product certifications are a plus (Administrator, Advanced Administrator) * Experience with Partner Program Operations and Support * Fluency in English and one or more other languages including Spanish is a plus; * LI\-Y Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form . Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. Posting Statement Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: CM: ESB Trainee Solicitor 2026
description: Vacancy Number: 28579 Category: Apprentice \- Business Job Type: Temporary Full\-time **Trainee Solicitor Contract with the ESB in\-house Legal Team** **Position Description** ESB’s in\-house Legal Department plays a pivotal role in delivering ESB’s *Net Zero by 2040* strategy. The team advises across all of ESB’s businesses in electricity generation, supply, and transmission, providing expert legal support in six core practice areas: Corporate \& Commercial, Regulatory \& Competition, Employment \& Industrial Relations, Litigation \& Dispute Resolution, Commercial Property, and Planning \& Environmental law. As a member of the in\-house legal team, the trainee solicitor will contribute directly to the delivery of ESB’s strategy, which is anchored in ESB’s ambition to lead the national transition to a low‑carbon energy future based on clean, reliable, and affordable electricity. ESB’s strategy involves significant capital investment across multiple complex projects, requiring the provision of timely, high‑quality legal advice and transactional support. The trainee solicitor will work across the ESB Legal Department, undertaking rotations in a range of the practice areas outlined above. During each legal practice rotation, the trainee solicitor will report to a senior solicitor within the relevant practice area. In addition, a designated training solicitor will be appointed to provide ongoing guidance, support, and supervision throughout the training contract. **Key Responsibilities of this role** The responsibilities of the successful candidate will include: * Working proactively and collaboratively with the senior practice area solicitor or a delegated solicitor to provide high quality and timely legal advice to internal clients across ESB Group * Assisting in the management of files to ensure that client’s needs are attended to in a prompt manner with clear high quality legal advice * Assisting with the drafting of correspondence and contracts * Research and advisory work in collaboration with senior practice area solicitor * Meeting with and advising ESB internal clients in collaboration with the senior practice area solicitor * Working in compliance with and ensuring adherence with ESB’s corporate governance frameworks * Liaising with other specialist areas within ESB as required * Assisting in maintaining positive and collaborative relationships with internal clients, the ESB in\-house legal team, external law firms and counsel appointed by ESB * Keeping appraised of legislative changes and case law **Essential Skills, Experience and Qualifications** The successful candidate will: * Demonstrate strong organisational and analytical skills * Possess effective communication and interpersonal abilities * Show excellent legal research and drafting capability * Work collaboratively and build strong relationships with clients and colleagues * Act with integrity and professionalism * Perform effectively under pressure and meet tight deadlines * Be proficient in Microsoft 365 **Qualification** On or before 30th July 2026: Has successfully passed all eight Law Society of Ireland FE1 entrance examinations and meet the Law Society of Ireland’s requirements for acceptance onto its Professional Practice Course 2026, commencing in September 2026 and passed all entry requirements to the Law Society After completion of the Professional Practice Course 2026 the trainee solicitor will be required to undertake the advanced electives. The training solicitor will provide guidance to the trainee solicitor on the electives that align best with ESB’s Driven to make a difference Net Zero by 2040 strategy. Subject to the terms of agreement between ESB and the trainee solicitor ESB will make payment of the trainee solicitors Professional Practice Course and Advanced Elective fees. The trainee solicitor will also receive a salary while undertaking the Professional Practice Course 2026\. **Location** The base location for this role will be ESB Networks Office, Leopardstown Road and/or ESB Head Office, 27 Fitzwilliam Street Lower, Dublin 2, D02KT92\. Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration **at least two days per week**. All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business. **Closing Date for applications:** **21st June 2026** **Salary:** **€39,000** ***Please advise if you require any additional accommodations to assist you if you are called to attend at interview.*** ***Note: Applicants who anticipate requiring assistance or reasonable accommodations for any part of the application or interview process may contact, in confidence, recruitment******@esb.ie*** ***ESB is an equal opportunity employer*** **Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Statement** ESB is committed to being an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all sections of society and ensure that no one is discriminated against on the grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnicity or nationality, disability, age, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by law. Applicants who anticipate requiring assistance or reasonable accommodations for any part of the application or interview process may contact, in confidence, recruitment@esb.ie. **About ESB** Join us in our mission to achieve a net\-zero electricity system by 2040\. ESB is Ireland’s leading energy utility, with activities spanning electricity generation, transmission and distribution, energy supply, energy services and international consultancy. Operating in Ireland, Northern Ireland and Great Britain, we invest over €1 billion each year to deliver a new energy future based on reliable, affordable zero\-carbon electricity.

title: Childcare Assistant Naionra Dhun Dealgan (Community Employment Scheme)
company: Dundalk Community Employment Childcare Project Ltd
description: **To be eligible to participate on a Community Employment scheme you must be 21 years or over and in receipt of an Irish Welfare Payment for 1 year or more.** **Applicants must provide suitable references and be prepared to undertake Garda Vetting.** Great opportunity to become a qualified Childcare Practitioner.If you are interested in a career in Childcare this programme is for you as we will provide the accredited training to help you achieve a major QQI award at Level 5 or Level 6 in Early Childhood Care and Education. No experience is necessary \- just a genuine interest in pursuing a career in Childcare. **Working in an Irish Language environment at the Naionra** **Dhun Dealgan, Muirhevnamor, Dundalk** , you will assist the manager and other qualified staff with daily childcare duties. The placement requires you to work 19\.5 hours per week over 5 mornings and may be available for up to 3 years so that you may gain your qualification and develop your childcare skills, in all the required childcare components, through training and hands on practical experience working with experienced and qualified staff in a community creche/playgroup For further information please call Dundalk CE Childcare Project Ltd on 042 9395005\. Pay: €14\.20\-€14\.40 per hour Work Location: In person

title: Cleaner
description: Where those who love pizza can be their true self. Benefits: * Free Food on shift * Plus tips \- You'll receive tips/Tronc\* * 50% off food and drink when you’re not working * Wellbeing Support for you and your family * Referral Bonus of €450 for referring pizza chefs and €1150 for referring managers * Pension \& Life Assurance * Pay Rate is €12\.80 per hour for under 20s and €13\.50 for age 20 and over * Learning and Development Opportunities to invest in you You’ll be the heart of our team, making sure that everything in our Pizzeria is spotless and restocked. You’ll create the perfect atmosphere in which our customers can have enlivening experiences and enjoy our famous pizza. Just be yourself and we will bring the best out of you. No experience required. What you’ll be doing: Run the pot wash, clean crockery and glassware and restock Clean floors and bathrooms Working in a dynamic and energetic environment Who we are: Where those who love pizza can be their true self. We’ve been around since 1965, bringing pizza to the UK and have since grown to a multichannel, international brand. Our people are at the heart of our success and everything we do; we believe you should always be able to express yourself. Here you can be yourself. Inclusivity and respect are at the heart of our business and are valued by our teams, customers, and suppliers. PizzaExpress and Milano is a place where everyone is welcome, and everyone belongs. That’s why we assess each application on the aptitude to do the job and nothing else. If you are invited to an interview, please let your interviewer know at the time of booking if you require any reasonable adjustments to be made for you. * Tronc, or card gratuities, is a fair way to pay gratuities to our teams. All Tronc goes to you, shared between our waiting and non\-waiting teams. You’ll get this at the end of each pay period, along with your normal pay and it’s one of the best perks about working in hospitality. Cleaner Dublin, County Dublin, Ireland D22 WA02 €12\.80 to €14\.83 per hour Permanent \- Full\-time Closing date: 17/06/2026

title: Cleaner
description: **About The Company:** OCS UK \& Ireland is a leading facilities management company with 50,000\+ colleagues and a turnover in excess of £2bn. We deliver innovative, award\-winning services within facilities management, hard services, cleaning, security and catering. Our mission is to make people and places the best they can be for our colleagues, customers and the communities we serve. Our commitment to doing business the right way is rooted in our TRUE values \- **Trust, Respect, Unity, and Empowerment.** **About The Role:** As a Cleaner at OCS, you will play a crucial role in providing safe, clean environments in which our customers can thrive in their workspaces. **Days of working:** Benefits for working with us: * Free Online Doctor Consultations via Web\-doctor * Competitive Salary * Contributory Pension after qualifying period * 21 Days Annual Leave increasing with service to 26 Days * Additional Leave Day on your Birthday after 1 year service * Staff Benefits/Discount Programme * Employee Assistance Programme * Training and Development Opportunities * Free onsite Parking **Your key responsibilities will include, but are not limited to:** * Tidying up work areas, cleaning floors and communal areas. * Vacuuming, sweeping, mopping, emptying bins and other tasks as required. * You may come into regular contact with customers so you must be able to assist them if needed. * You must complete all relevant Health \& Safety records and attend training courses when needed. **The ideal candidate should meet the following criteria:** * You must have Right to Work in the EU * A background in cleaning would be advantageous. * Attention to detail and thoroughness in completing tasks. **How to Apply:** If you are interested in joining a business that encourages professional development, career progression and the chance to work with inspirational people then please click apply and register your interest! You can also sign up to our job alerts when registering which will notify you when other suitable vacancies are available. We are an equal opportunities employer and rely on a diverse workforce with a broad range of knowledge, skills, and backgrounds to deliver our goals. We offer an inclusive and welcoming environment and actively encourage applications from all individuals regardless of race, gender, nationality, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or age.

title: Cloud Cost Optimization Engineer - 12 Month FTC
description: **Sustainability that means business** **Who we are:** Sustainability software specialist, AMCS, is headquartered in Ireland, with offices in Europe, the USA, and Australasia. With over 1,300 highly\-skilled employees across 22 countries, we specialize in delivering technology solutions to facilitate a carbon neutral future. **What we do:** Our innovative SaaS solutions increase efficiency and boost sustainability in resource\-intensive industries. Over 5,000 customers across 23 countries already benefit from our Performance Sustainability software, ensuring we deliver practical solutions for improved profitability and environmental resilience across the globe. **Our people:** AMCS offers team members more than just a job, but an opportunity to map out a career with a company that is growing, evolving and setting out new ways of working that are having a positive impact on the world around us. AMCS was established in Ireland and holds onto those local roots and ‘start\-up’ mentality with a culture of connection. Connection to our work, our customers, our colleagues and our community that creates a working environment that fosters openness, collaboration and creativity. **Role Purpose:** The Cloud Cost Optimization Engineer will drive financial accountability, cost optimization and operational efficiency across cloud platforms within the Cloud Operations organization. This role ensures visibility into cloud spend, implements cost\-effective provisioning strategies, and balances financial efficiency with engineering agility. **Key Responsibilities:** * Develop and execute a comprehensive FinOps roadmap aligned with organizational goals. * Establish governance policies for resource utilization, provisioning, and budget approvals. * Financial Accountability * Collaborate with Finance, Product, and Engineering teams to ensure cost transparency and budgeting discipline. * Manage vendor relationships (cloud providers, software suppliers), raise purchase orders, and ensure timely invoice processing. * Operational Excellence * Design and implement a FinOps observability platform to monitor cloud costs and usage. * Integrate cloud cost management tools into the SDLC. * Define and track KPIs for cloud cost optimization and efficiency. * Implement policies for instance rightsizing, resource provisioning, and decommissioning unused resources. * Drive automation initiatives for cost efficiency across cloud environments. * Education Awareness * Promote FinOps best practices across engineering teams. * Conduct training sessions to improve cost awareness and financial literacy within technical teams. **Required Skills Experience:** * Proven experience in FinOps, Cloud Operations, or Cloud Financial Management. * Strong understanding of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and associated cost models. * Experience with cost management tools. * Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills. * Ability to design governance frameworks and implement automation for cost optimization. **Preferred Qualifications:** * FinOps Certified Practitioner or similar certification. * Background in DevOps, SRE, or Cloud Architecture. * Familiarity with budgeting and financial processes in large organizations \#LI\-IB1

title: Cloud Engineer
description: **Microsoft 365 Cloud Infrastructure Support Engineer** **(Microsoft Graph Essential)** **Permanent** **Dublin, Hybrid** My client a multinational organisation is on the lookout for a Microsoft365 Cloud Infrastructure Support Engineer on an permanent basis in Dublin. **The Job:** * Infrastructure, Apps \& Automation Ownership: Design, build, and maintain low\-code solutions and end\-to\-end automation workflows using Power Apps and Power Automate. Leverage Microsoft Graph to streamline enterprise identity, user onboarding/offboarding, licensing, and system operations. * Modern Workplace \& Endpoint Engineering: Build, configure, and support robust Windows 11 endpoint environments. Manage modern endpoint management tools (Intune and/or SCCM) to handle application deployments, compliance policies, and configuration profiles. * Lifecycle \& Asset Management: Support end\-to\-end device provisioning, onboarding, and lifecycle management (including hardware refresh cycles, device inventory, and asset management). Oversee the setup, maintenance, and ergonomic standards of user workstations, laptops, monitors, docking stations, and peripherals. * Security \& Identity Governance: Implement and maintain stringent endpoint security controls, OS hardening, and protection standards. Administer Entra ID, Conditional Access, MFA, and device compliance policies to ensure a secure posture. * Tier\-3 Systems Escalation: Act as the senior escalation point from the Service Desk for EUC and Microsoft 365 core services (Teams, Exchange Online, SharePoint, OneDrive). Provide advanced, independent troubleshooting across hardware, operating systems, networking (DNS, DHCP, VPN, Wi\-Fi), and application layers. * Operations \& Documentation: Actively monitor service health, support EUC/M365\-related projects, and create/maintain comprehensive technical documentation and runbooks. **Have You:** * EUC \& Endpoint Experience: Spent 3\-5 years in End User Computing, Desktop Engineering, or Endpoint Support roles, with strong hands\-on experience managing Windows 11 environments? * Microsoft Certifications: Earned relevant Microsoft certifications demonstrating your expertise? *(e.g., Microsoft 365 Certified: Endpoint Administrator Associate, Enterprise Administrator Expert, or Power Platform certifications)* * Power Platform \& API Automation: Developed custom enterprise apps in Power Apps and built production\-ready workflows using Power Automate, directly interacting with the Microsoft Graph API **(Essential)** to automate manual administrative and identity tasks * Modern Management \& Security: Proven your ability to manage modern endpoint tools (Intune/SCCM), deploy applications, enforce encryption/compliance, and implement OS hardening standards * M365 \& Entra ID Competency: Demonstrated deep experience administering Microsoft 365 services (Teams, Exchange, OneDrive) alongside Entra ID, Conditional Access, and MFA * Hardware \& Infrastructure Troubleshooting: Mastered advanced troubleshooting across hardware, peripheral devices, operating systems, and basic networking concepts * Senior Delivery \& Communication: Proven your ability to work independently as a senior escalation resource, combining strong communication skills with a highly customer\-focused approach **Berkley Business \& Technology is a specialist consultancy, recruiting professionals for organizations throughout Ireland, UK \& Europe; for more information go to www.berkley\-group.com.** **If you would like to discuss this vacancy further or to discuss your career options in confidence, please send your profile to brendanoconnell@berkley\-group.com**

title: Commercial Graduate Sales Path - German Market (August start)
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* **About Futureforce University Recruiting** Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more. **Job Category** Sales **Job Details** **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Fixed\-term contract** **Duration:** 12 months **Start Date:** August 2026 We are thrilled to invite ambitious graduate candidates who would like to build a career in **Salesforce** to apply for the **5th cohort** of our **EMEA Graduate Rotation Program – Sales Path** . This exciting **12\-month program** is designed to give graduates exposure to both sales and solution engineering. Having built the foundational skills for both roles and met our eligibility criteria, top graduates on this path can begin their **Salesforce Sales** careers. The promotion window opens from **month 7 onwards.** Our graduate program is a fully immersive experience, combining comprehensive learning paths with in\-role experience. You will be assigned experienced **Sales Development Buddys** who will provide hands\-on coaching, invite you to join their discovery and qualification calls, giving you the confidence to pick up the phone to reach out to your first salesforce customers. You will also be paired with experienced **Solution Engineer Buddies** during the SE Rotation who will involve you in their live deals. Throughout the programme, you will gain exposure to senior sales and solution engineering professionals during our inspiration sessions. By joining Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundation for a **fast\-paced, successful career** . We are a proud member of the **Fortune 100 Best Places to Work** list for the past 14 years and a consistent winner of **Great Place to Work** awards. Who are we looking for? * You are a 2025 or 2026 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree) from all academic backgrounds * You are interested in the impact technology can have on the world and business * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * You are curious, self\-motivated, and focused on continuous learning * You thrive in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-oriented and comfortable in a fast\-paced environment * You are fluent in English \& German What does our EMEA Graduate Program look like? **The Program Is Structured As Follows** Introduction Month During your first month, you will receive extensive onboarding enablement through a Salesforce\-style academy, where you will: * Become immersed in the culture of Salesforce and gain a deep understanding of our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies, and tools * Learn critical professional skills for your career at Salesforce * Follow a structured learning calendar with sessions and hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni Rotations During the Graduate Program, graduates on both the Sales and Solution Engineering Paths will follow the same curriculum and complete the same rotations. Essentially you will gain exposure to both the **Sales and Solution Engineering Roles** . The structure of each rotation is similar: you will spend **60% of your time** immersed in the role with clear tasks, targets, and stakeholder alignment, while the remaining **40%** will focus on role\-specific learning. You will be paired with experienced buddies in each path. If your first rotation (months 2 through 4\) is in **Sales** , you will then join the **Solution Engineering Rotation** from months 5 through 7, and vice versa. Over these six months, you will have built the fundamental skills for these complementary disciplines. Having gone deeper on your solution knowledge, learning how to demo and having exposure to later stages of the sales cycle during the solution engineering rotation will set you up for sucess as you promote throught the various sales roles \- SDR, BDR, AE and beyond. Throughout the rotations, there will be **self\-paced certifications and skills assessments** . You must meet all eligibility criteria before you can **panel for promotion** . Examples of what you can expect during the rotations **Sales Graduate Rotation** * Learn Salesforce’s best\-in\-class prospecting and qualification methodologies * Learn how to use social, digital, video, chat, and phone platforms to effectively engage Salesforce prospects * Collaborate with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organisation Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation * Learn our solutions at a deeper, more technical level * Work closely with the Solution Engineering organisation, supporting the creation of cutting\-edge customer demonstrations * Learn the art of customer discovery and how to create a compelling customer point of view What’s in it for you? * A clear and structured career path within our Sales organisation * The opportunity to work in an international team within a nurturing environment * Access to our diverse internal employee groups * A comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance throughout your relocation process * An additional seven days of paid time off for charitable causes – learn more about our 1\-1\-1 model Map your future for the coming years With the **EMEA Graduate Program – Sales Path** , you can continue to progress: * After your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Sales Development Representative, Business Development Representative and then Account Executive, where you will position our solutions to potential customers. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong mentoring culture, we strive to develop industry\-leading Solution Engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to build international experience within an organisation that has transformed the enterprise software market through cloud computing. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you require assistance due to a disability applying for open positions please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. ********Posting Statement******** Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Commercial Technology Associate
description: We are looking for talented commercially\-focused graduates wishing to pursue a career in Technology. The successful candidate will focus initially on sales and marketing support, lead generation, and outbound prospecting, but will ultimately develop into an Account Executive, managing opportunities from initial contact to contract signature and relationship management. This position is based in our Dublin office. The ideal candidate will be innovative, driven and highly motivated to succeed. They will be client\-focused with excellent communication skills and a ‘can\-do’ attitude. The ability to multitask and work independently are key requirements, while ambition and diligence are expected as standard. This is an exciting opportunity which will allow the successful candidate to not only develop and refine their sales skills but to have a direct impact on the strategy of the team. **This role involves:** •Finding relevant contacts and reaching out in a systematic and personalised manner via email, LinkedIn, and phone. •Setting up meetings, qualifying opportunities, and guiding prospects through our established sales process. •Collaborating with Marketing to ensure a constant flow of quality leads. •Working towards an annual sales target. •Account Management, Managed Services and Operational Support activities for existing clients. **Abilities \& Skills:** •Positive 'can\-do’ attitude and self\-starter mentality. •Enthusiastic, confident and professional manner. •Ability to listen to clients and understand their needs. •An interest in technology and cybersecurity. •Curious and a continuous learner. •Highly organized with an ability to plan and prioritise your day effectively. **Minimum Requirements:** •A strong academic record, encompassing a third\-level qualification. •Excellent communication and organisational skills with the ability to multitask. •Previous experience in a sales\-driven role would be a distinct advantage. **Additional Skills:** •Willingness to travel. •Strong negotiation skills. •Ambitious, determined and hard\-working. Intuition is an Equal Opportunities Employer

title: Community Cognitive Stimulation Therapist (CST)
description: We are seeking a compassionate and skilled **Community Cognitive Stimulation Therapy (CST) Nurse or Allied Health Professional** to lead our outpatient group interventions for individuals living with **mild\-to\-moderate dementia**. Based in the community, this role focuses on delivering evidence\-based psychosocial interventions to enhance cognitive functioning, promote social inclusion, and support independent living. * **Job Title:** Community Cognitive Stimulation Therapist (CST) * **Setting:** Outpatient Community Clinics / Day Services * **Target Patient Group:** Adults living in the community with mild\-to\-moderate dementia * **Position Type:** Part\-Time 7\.3 hours p/week (Specify hours) **Key Responsibilities** **Group Facilitation \& Clinical Delivery** * **Lead CST Groups:** Facilitate structured, 7\-week evidence\-based CST group cycles (14 themed sessions) for community\-dwelling adults. * **Tailor Activities:** Adapt multi\-sensory activities, word games, music, and current affairs discussions to match the cognitive abilities of the group. * **Promote Connection:** Foster a welcoming, non\-judgmental atmosphere that reduces social isolation and encourages meaningful peer interaction. * **Maintain Consistency:** Establish a predictable, secure group routine to minimize anxiety and build participant confidence. **Assessment \& Community Caseload Management** * **Screen Referrals:** Evaluate community, GP, and memory clinic referrals to assess suitability for outpatient CST groups. * **Track Outcomes:** Perform baseline and post\-programme cognitive, mood, and quality\-of\-life assessments using standardized tools. * **Monitor Wellbeing:** Watch for signs of physical decline, delirium, or caregiver burnout, making onward referrals when necessary. * **Document Care:** Maintain accurate, up\-to\-date clinical notes regarding patient attendance, engagement, and progression. **Family Support \& Multidisciplinary Collaboration** * **Support Carers:** Provide resources and education to family members, helping them extend cognitive stimulation techniques into the home. * **Community Awareness:** Act as an advocate for dementia\-friendly community initiatives and promote the benefits of early psychosocial intervention. **Qualifications \& Requirements** **Education \& Professional Licensure** * **Graduate Degree:** Registered General (RGN)/Registered Psychiatric/Mental Health Nurse (RPN) or Allied Health Professional (OT / Social Worker / Physiotherapist). * **Active Registration:** Current, valid registration with the Nursing and Midwifery Board of Ireland (NMBI) or equivalent regulatory body. **Experience \& Specialised Training** * **Dementia Care:** Minimum of 2 years of post\-qualification experience working with older adults or community dementia services. * **CST Certification:** Certified training in Cognitive Stimulation Therapy facilitation is highly desirable (training will be provided to the ideal candidate). **Core Competencies** * **Communication:** Masterful verbal and non\-verbal communication skills adapted for individuals with progressive cognitive impairment. * **Group Management:** Proven ability to manage group dynamics, balance varying participation levels, and de\-escalate responsive behaviours. * **Independence:** Strong organizational and time\-management skills required to coordinate community outpatient clinics efficiently. **Full, clean driving licence required.**

title: Compliance Associate
description: **Compliance Advisory Associate:** Our client, a Globally Significant Asset Manager, are looking for a talented compliance advisory professional to join their team in **Dublin,** as an Associate. The role will be helping to provide compliance advisory services covering portfolio management, trading, sales and marketing activity within the Group, working with senior stakeholders across the EMEA jurisdiction. **Key Responsibilities:** * Provide Compliance support and representation on Group strategic initiatives. * Provide daily compliance surveillance of Group portfolios for regulatory requirements and applicable policies and procedures as well as drafting policies and procedures. * Compliance advisory regarding global asset management marketing and distribution questions: UCITS/Non\-UCITS/cross border distribution. **Key Requirements:** * 3 years regulatory risk or compliance experience (asset management experience preferred). * General compliance knowledge and familiarity with CBI and FCA regulations, MiFID, UCITS, AIFMD and SFDR. * Knowledge of investment instruments including derivatives. This is a **permanent** role based in Dublin and will require being fully on\-site.

title: Creative Automation Specialist
description: ### **Creative Automation Specialist** We are looking for a tech savvy marketeer to join our team at Flutter UK \& Ireland. As a member of our MarTech team in Marketing Services, we want you to put automation at the heart of our workflow processes and drive forward our AI /automation capabilities across key digital marketing channels to improve efficiency, targeting, optimisation and campaign delivery. The successful candidate will work with the wider Marketing Services team to best apply operational excellence across key tooling partners in the delivery of our marketing programme across brands and verticals. ### **What you’ll do** * Manage and optimise creative automation platforms ensuring compatibility with our strategic marketing partnerships (DCO, templating, dynamic content tools). * Partner with design and media teams to build scalable templates and dynamic creative variations enhancing the marketing workflow process of the future. * Implement workflows for creative scale that enable rapid creative versioning, localisation, and testing that enhance creative effectveness. * Ensure creative assets are structured, tagged, and delivered efficiently across channels; operationalising key digital asset management solutions delivered by our product \& technology colleagues. * Be curious, seeking new ways to deliver creative innovation and automation and keeping Flutter at the forefront of marketing excellence. * Analyse creative performance and recommend automation\-led improvements for speed, relevance, and impact in both a customer and commercial driven context. ### **How you’ll do it:** * Bachelors degree in Marketing, Business Communications or Applied Computer Science (preferred) * Exposure to marketing automation platforms with familiarity of basic marketing metrics. Hands\-on experience using AI platforms such as OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, or similar technologies in a marketing context. * Exposure to creative tooling and automation solutions (Digital Asset Management, Content Management platforms akin StoryTeq, Video Automation, CMS) * Experience building or supporting content creation and customer journey at scale * Working knowledge of HTML/CSS and operating systems * Comply with responsible AI, governance frameworks, \& AI ethics. * Experience working in multi\-location and cross\-functional teams in a multi brand and multi vertical business. * A good understanding of Gen AI, large language models, and how AI products work. You stay current with AI developments and can articulate how AI capabilities can solve business problems. You actively leverage Flutter’s AI tools to maximize your productivity. ### **About Flutter UK \& Ireland** ### **Extraordinary starts here.** Flutter UK \& Ireland unites a dozen brands, hundreds of teams and thousands of colleagues who create trusted entertainment for millions of customers every week. With the likes of Paddy Power, Betfair and Sky Betting \& Gaming in the fold, we’re the largest division of global entertainment giant Flutter and boast some of the most popular apps and games in UK and Ireland markets. At Flutter UK\&I, we can guarantee you that no two days will ever be the same — and you’ll have the chance to make a difference to your career, the industry, and your amazing colleagues. ### **What we’ll offer you** * £1,000 annual learning fund * Unlimited holiday * Twice\-yearly bonus (with part of it guaranteed!) * Pension contribution scheme * Private healthcare * Flexible ways of working – home or office, it’s your choice! * Access to thousands of Udemy courses * Invest via the Company Sharesave Scheme * 16 hours paid volunteering time per year **We’re working to be an inclusive employer, and we encourage people from all backgrounds, ways of thinking and working to apply.** **Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences; you don't have to meet all the requirements listed to apply for this role.**

title: Customer Service Associate
description: **ABOUT GREYSTAR** Greystar is a leading, fully integrated global real estate platform offering expertise in property management, investment management, development, and construction services in institutional\-quality rental housing. Headquartered in Charleston, South Carolina, Greystar manages and operates over $300 billion of real estate in more than 265 markets globally with offices throughout North America, Europe, South America, and the Asia\-Pacific region. Greystar is the largest operator of apartments in the United States, managing over one million units/beds globally. Across its platforms, Greystar has nearly $79 billion of assets under management, including over $35 billion of development assets and over $36\.5 billion of regulatory assets under management. Greystar was founded by Bob Faith in 1993 to become a provider of world\-class service in the rental residential real estate business. To learn more, visit www.greystar.com. **JOB DESCRIPTION SUMMARY** Works as part of a team to provide day\-to\-day management of the property and its community including marketing, building maintenance and tenancy administration and promotes and maintains an excellent resident experience in order to achieve occupancy and retention goals.**JOB DESCRIPTION** ***Key Role Responsibilities*** * Works as part of a team, supporting and respecting other team members, to deliver exceptional resident living. * Supports the creation of a positive, memorable experience for residents * Delivers all aspects of customer service, including a comprehensive front of house service anticipating and exceeding resident expectations. * Responds positively to customer queries and complaints, identifying and undertaking appropriate action in line with Greystar’s complaints procedure. * Supports events and activities within the Community. * Establishes and maintains relationships with University clients. * Develops and maintains local knowledge and information resources relevant to resident needs i.e. travel, entertainment and amenities. * Completes administrative tasks including logging of maintenance requests, filing and preparing notices and updating of databases. * Undertakes marketing activities such as attending open days and leafleting to promote the property. * Carries out sales and leasing activities including: viewings, following up on enquiries and sales conversions. * Assists with summer Community preparations, including move\-in and move\-out processes. * Completes Health and Safety compliance activities in line with the Company’s policies and procedures. * Participates where required in an on call roster to provide out of hours emergency support for purchase orders in accordance with procedures * Ensure tenant refunds are completed in a timely manner and in line with Greystar’s policy. * Look to maximise efficiency of utilities. ***Important title: Customer Support Engineer
description: **Company Overview** KLA is a global leader in diversified electronics for the semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem. No laptop, smartphone, wearable device, voice\-controlled gadget, flexible screen, VR device or smart car would have made it into your hands without us. KLA invents systems and solutions for the manufacturing of wafers and reticles, integrated circuits, packaging, printed circuit boards and flat panel displays. The innovative ideas and devices that are advancing humanity all begin with inspiration, research and development. KLA focuses more than average on innovation and we invest 15% of sales back into R\&D. Our expert teams of physicists, engineers, data scientists and problem\-solvers work together with the world’s leading technology providers to accelerate the delivery of tomorrow’s electronic devices. Life here is exciting and our teams thrive on tackling really hard problems. This role also assures operational quality of system equipment and will coordinate actions with customers to minimize down time. Responsibilities: * Evaluates, analyses, diagnoses and troubleshoots technical equipment problems at customer site. Provides guidance and technical assistance to Installation Engineer on installations at customer sites or on installing/dismantling demonstration machines. Preferred Experience / skills * Demonstrates expert level of analytical problem solving; Capability to determine root cause, provide relevant information and help to develop prevention techniques. * Expert abilities in troubleshooting software and electronic in all sub\-systems and systems. * Develops capabilities in use of all available diagnostic resources. System expert in all aspects of assigned equipment set. * Ability to address overall system issues (hardware, software, networking, automation, applications); ability to drive customer improvement programs, change order programs. * Proficient in general and factory automation on assigned equipment set. * Capability to work with Tech Support and Engineering in special projects and/or escalations * Proficiency in systems level repair of multiple or extremely complex products within a family is required. * Strong interpersonal/communication skills in understanding customer needs. * Ability to work under pressure and stringent timelines. * Ideally hold an Engineering qualification in Electrical or Electro / Mechanical discipline or have equivalent level of experience. **Minimum Qualifications** Engineering qualification in Electrical, Electro / Mechanical or similar discipline or have equivalent level of experience *We offer a competitive, family friendly total rewards package. We design our programs to reflect our commitment to an inclusive environment, while ensuring we provide benefits that meet the diverse needs of our employees.* *KLA is proud to be an equal opportunity employer* Be aware of potentially fraudulent job postings or suspicious recruiting activity by persons that are currently posing as KLA employees. Further, KLA does not work with any recruiters or third parties who charge such fees either directly or on behalf of KLA. Please ensure that you have searched KLA’s Careers website for legitimate job postings. KLA follows a recruiting process that involves multiple interviews in person or on video conferencing with our hiring managers. If you are concerned that a communication, an interview, an offer of employment, or that an employee is not legitimate, please send an email to talent.acquisition@kla.com to confirm the person you are communicating with is an employee. We take your privacy very seriously and confidentially handle your information.

title: Data Center Operations Engineer - Dublin
description: Technology Data Center Operations Engineer \- Dublin Location : Dublin Employment Type : Regular Job Code : A158714 Responsibilities **Team Insight:** The DataCenter Service / Datacenter Cloud System (DCS) team sits within TikTok's global technology structure and supports the company's fast growth by building and operating hyper\-scale datacenters, managing the life cycle of server fleet, providing cloud solutions, and developing various infrastructure services, making sure they are scalable and are reliable. **Role Insight:** We are seeking an experienced Data Center Operations Engineers to apply technical expertise in a dynamic, fast\-paced environment. This role requires strong knowledge of server hardware and a foundational understanding of mechanical and electrical infrastructure in large\-scale data centers. You will be responsible for diagnosing and resolving server and infrastructure issues, collaborating with remote teams, and supporting the full server rack lifecycle, including buildout of compute and storage environments. In addition, you will lead and guide DC Technicians, ensuring high\-quality execution of operational tasks and adherence to best practices. Candidates should have hands\-on experience in at least one of the following areas: Networking, Scripting, or Hardware Repair. Success in this role requires strong communication skills, the ability to work independently and within a team, and adaptability in a rapidly changing environment. Responsibilities * Own and manage ticket queues, prioritizing by business impact and ensuring timely resolution or escalation with clear documentation * Lead day\-to\-day operations by guiding Data Center Technicians and overseeing hardware troubleshooting, maintenance, and diagnostics (including component replacement, power cycles, BIOS/RAID, OS reimaging, and log analysis using internal tools) * Execute and coordinate rack\-and\-stack, cabling, and deployment activities across the server lifecycle * Support data center projects and operational improvements, including capacity expansions, retrofits, infrastructure upgrades, and implementation of new tools and processes * Participate in incident management and incident response, recovery, and root cause analysis to minimize service impact and prevent recurrence. This requires participating in an on\-call rotation for escalations * Lead and apply change management processes, acting as a change initiator where required * Assist on maintaining accurate inventory, asset tracking, and operational records * Build strong cross\-functional relationships, act as an escalation point, and identify recurring issues to drive vendor escalation and continuous improvement Qualifications Minimum Qualifications * Associate’s degree in IT, Computer Science, Electrical/Mechanical Engineering, or related field (or equivalent practical experience) * 3\+ years of experience in data center operations, IT infrastructure, or hardware support * Relevant certification or equivalent knowledge (e.g., EPI CDCP, CNET CDCTP or similar) * Strong hands\-on experience with server hardware troubleshooting and support * Ability to lead tasks and guide junior technicians in a fast\-paced environment Preferred Qualifications * Industry certifications (CompTIA A\+, Network\+, Server\+, Linux\+ or similar) * Experience in large\-scale data center environments * Basic knowledge of mechanical and electrical infrastructure systems * Experience with networking, scripting, or advanced hardware repair * Experience supporting projects, incident management, or small team leadership * Experience operating material handling equipment (e.g., pallet jacks, server lifts) Job Information About TikTok TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo. ​ Why Join Us Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day. ​ We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us. ​ ​ Diversity \& Inclusion ​ TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

title: Data Centre Operative
description: ATC is a European logistics, white glove, and technical services specialist for data centres. Our focus is on anticipating the critical needs of our clients and delivering industry\-leading standards for safety, security and reliability. Since being established in 1979, we have continued to lead our dedicated teams across Europe to build strong relationships with our clients. Our mission is to deliver and install Europe’s data centres with precision and care. We achieve this by focusing on our team and our services: * **Our Team** – Powered by people to keep data centres moving safely and securely. We recognise that our greatest strength is our staff, which is why we promote a culture of personal development and professional training, both internally and from leading industry bodies. * **Our Services** – We provide a range of specialised services tailored to the individual needs and demands of each client, their equipment, and their data centre infrastructure. From managing specialised computer transport, and IT relocation services, to logistics and planning, and data centre commissioning and decommissioning; we adapt our 40\+ years of expertise to every situation. ATC operates in a dynamic global market where clients demand a service that is right first time, every time; we are recruiting motivated and driven people to work in a company that is *Driving Forward.* We are looking for hands\-on reliable people who are flexible, adaptable and prepared to work with our clients to join our exciting and skilled team installing hardware and server equipment at client sites, while delivering a first class service. If you’ve a background in Warehousing, Logistics, Construction, or a similar physical job, your skills are a perfect fit — and we’ll provide the hardware and security training you need. **Requirements** **What you’ll bring:** * Proven experience in a practical, hands\-on role * Valid Class B driving licence * Comfortable using basic hand and power tools * Able to use (or learn to use) simple computer tools like Excel or Teams * Basic English and local language skills (B1\) **Nice to have (but not essential):** * Experience in warehousing, logistics, construction, or data centres * Willingness to travel for work * Any previous cabling or installation experience **Why join us?** * A team that values safety, teamwork, and professionalism * Full training and support provided * Clear progression opportunities * Fantastic facilities **What you’ll do:** * Travel to client sites with our teams, with the required PPE and tools. * Unload, move, and set up hardware equipment (like server racks) * Help with cabling and equipment setup (training provided) * Follow site safety and security procedures * Keep clear communication with your team and complete simple checklists This role provides candidates with opportunities to learn and develop. There are pathways to delivery supervisor, cabling technician, or team leader, and training and mentorship are provided. Candidates can also travel with work as part of ATC’s Mobilisation Team. **Benefits** The remunerating package, including salary and benefits, is negotiable depending on experience. The package may include the following: * + Competitive market salary + Performance\-based bonus + AIG Virtual Care Programme + Company pension plan + Sports \& social club + Opportunities for continuing professional development **Note: We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this role.** **Please submit your updated resume/CV in English.**

title: DevOps Engineer
description: JOB DESCRIPTION At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts. Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet. Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all. **About the team and the role** ------------------------------- Native Platform Engineering owns the end\-to\-end systems that build, test, sign, and release eBay’s global iOS and Android applications. Our Mobile DevOps team operates a distributed CI/CD platform composed of high\-throughput Jenkins and GitHub Actions pipelines, large\-scale macOS/Xcode and Android build fleets, hybrid cloud and on\-prem compute, and production\-grade observability systems. We approach this as a systems engineering problem: throughput, determinism, isolation, fault domains, capacity modeling, and secure supply chain design all matter. As a DevOps Engineer (SE3\), you will contribute to the reliability and evolution of this shared platform, improving subsystems while ensuring they integrate cleanly into the broader architecture. You’ll work closely with peers across platform, infrastructure, security, and mobile engineering to strengthen the whole system rather than operate in isolation. This role reports to the DevOps Engineering Manager and is based on\-site in Dublin. **What you will accomplish** ---------------------------- * **I**mprove system throughput and determinism across CI/CD pipelines by reducing flakiness, optimizing parallelization, and improving cache efficiency and stage isolation. * Increase fleet reliability and performance through capacity planning, autoscaling strategies, workload scheduling, and failure\-domain analysis across macOS and Android runners. * Strengthen observability across subsystems by instrumenting metrics and logs, building actionable dashboards, and improving time\-to\-detection and time\-to\-recovery. * Enhance software supply chain integrity by advancing secure code signing, secrets management, artifact validation, and automated security controls within CI workflows. * Contribute to operational excellence by participating in incident response, performing root cause analysis, and implementing systemic reliability improvements. * Collaborate across engineering domains to evolve platform architecture, share knowledge, and continuously improve developer productivity at scale. **What you will bring** ----------------------- * Experience engineering or operating production CI/CD systems (e.g., Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI) with an understanding of pipeline architecture and failure modes. * Strong systems\-level diagnostic skills in Linux and macOS environments, including command\-line diagnostics and performance analysis. * Proficiency in automation and scripting (e.g., Python, Bash) and familiarity with infrastructure automation concepts. * Working knowledge of distributed systems fundamentals, including networking (TCP/IP, DNS, load balancing), resource isolation, and secure system design. * Experience with cloud infrastructure (Azure, GCP, or equivalent) and an understanding of scalability and fault tolerance principles. We value engineers who think in terms of systems, constraints, and trade\-offs. If you are motivated by improving complex engineering platforms that enable teams to ship reliably at global scale, we encourage you to apply. **Additional Details** eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines. AWS/OCI a plus) Container \& Kubernetes Security Strong understanding of Kubernetes security controls (RBAC, Secrets, Ingress, TLS) Familiarity with container runtime security and orchestration Technical Skills Proficiency in scripting (Python preferred; Bash or Go is a plus) Working knowledge of Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and automation Security Frameworks \& Compliance Knowledge of ISO 27001, SOC 2, NIST, and CIS Benchmarks Certifications (Preferred) CISSP CKS (Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist) GCP Professional Cloud Security Engineer Microsoft Azure Security Engineer / Cybersecurity Architect AWS Certified Security – Specialty Soft Skills Strong stakeholder management and ability to influence engineering teams Experience working in global or multi\-national environments ⭐ Nice to Have Experience securing microservices, APIs, and serverless architectures Background in penetration testing or red teaming (cloud/container focus) Experience in large\-scale SaaS, networking, or cloud\-first organizations Additional certifications across GCP, Azure, AWS, or OCI Kubernetes certifications such as CKA or advanced security\-focused credentials If interested kindly share your updated cv to remya\-sara@dyneits.com

title: Digital Automation Specialist
description: ### **Digital Automation Specialist** We are looking for a tech savvy marketeer to join our team at Flutter UK \& Ireland. You will be involved in HRIS and payroll data migration, data cleansing, validation, reporting, audit support and process improvement. The environment is fast\-moving and evolving, so this role would suit someone adaptable, resilient and comfortable working through ambiguity while maintaining a high level of accuracy. Key Responsibilities * Manage, maintain and validate HR data across multiple systems. * Support the transition from BambooHR and Gusto into Rippling. * Conduct regular HR data audits and identify inconsistencies, gaps or risks. * Support HRIS and payroll data migration projects. * Validate HR, payroll and finance\-related data to ensure accuracy and compliance. * Work with HR, payroll, finance and systems teams to improve workflows and system connectivity. * Support access management, permissions and custom field configuration within HR systems. * Assist with payroll and accounts payable\-related administration and reconciliation work. * Support reporting, budgeting and compliance tracking activities. * Help improve HR data processes, documentation standards and audit trails. * Proactively identify opportunities to make HR data processes more scalable, accurate and reliable. Requirements * 2–4 years’ experience in a HR data, HR operations, HRIS, payroll, compliance or data\-focused role. * Strong analytical and numerical ability. * Experience working with HR systems, payroll data or finance\-related processes. * Previous exposure to payroll and/or accounts payable would be highly advantageous. * Strong attention to detail and a high level of accuracy. * Ability to independently check, validate and sense\-check data. * Comfortable working with evolving systems, processes and transformation projects. * Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities. * Excellent communication skills and ability to work across HR, payroll and finance teams. * Experience with Rippling, BambooHR or Gusto would be advantageous. * Must be based in Ireland. * Fluent English essential. The Ideal Person This role would suit someone who: * Enjoys working with data, systems and process improvement. * Has a logical, structured and analytical approach. * Takes ownership and follows tasks through to completion. * Is compliance\-focused and detail\-oriented. * Enjoys problem solving and improving processes. * Can work well in a fast\-paced, scaling environment. * Is proactive, dependable and comfortable working independently. Additional Information * **Job Title:** HR Data Analyst * **Location:** Remote, Ireland * **Contract:** Initial 6\-month contract * **Start Date:** Immediate availability or short notice preferred * **Industry:** International technology / blockchain * **Working Model:** Fully remote
